title: Streaming two-phase analysis for CSV
rationale: First pass detects encoding/delimiter/header. Second pass classifies types and profiles data. Avoids loading entire file into memory. Enables handling large files.

title: Destination-centric transformation mappings
rationale: Every destination field gets a row. source_type discriminator: field, null, constant, unmapped. SuggestMappings defaults unmatched nullable fields to null, non-nullable to unmapped. This ensures no destination constraint is violated silently.

title: DataContract > SchemaContract > FieldDefinition type hierarchy
rationale: All shared types live in contract/ package. DataContract wraps one or more SchemaContracts (tables, endpoints, file sections). Each SchemaContract has FieldDefinitions with optional profiles and constraints. Single source of truth imported by all analyzers.

title: TransformationWithContext validates against destination constraints
rationale: Cross-reference validation checks NOT NULL, PK, FK, UQ constraints on destination fields. Error messages include field names and constraint types. Catches mapping errors before data moves.

title: Shared profile package extracted from csvcontract
rationale: Type classification, column profiling, header detection, and range tracking extracted into profile/ package for reuse by csvcontract and excelcontract. Uses contract.TopValue directly to avoid duplicate types. jsoncontract untouched since its type system differs.

title: Multi-source/multi-destination transformation contracts (v0.8.0)
rationale: Breaking change: Contract uses SourceRefs/DestinationRefs (plural) and MappingGroups instead of singular refs and flat FieldMappings. Each MappingGroup targets one destination schema. FieldMapping gains SourceRef to qualify which source. SuggestMappings accepts NamedSourceFields for multi-source matching.
